sanskrit: चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम् । तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥
hindiTranslation: क्योंकि हे कृष्ण  मन बड़ा ही चञ्चल, प्रमथनशील, दृढ़ जिद्दी और बलवान् है ।  उसका निग्रह करना मैं वायुकी तरह अत्यन्त कठिन मानता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: The mind is restless, turbulent, stubborn, and strong. I think controlling it is as hard as controlling the wind.
essence: The mind resists control like wind resists the hand.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna admits that the mind is wild, forceful, and hard to restrain, almost like trying to catch the wind.
simpleInsight: It feels honest to admit how hard inner control can be.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna has just asked Krishna about the steady state of meditation. He now confesses the real obstacle: the mind itself. This verse deepens the problem before Krishna answers with a practical method.
modernRelevance: You sit down to pray, meditate, or even make one clean decision, and your thoughts scatter in every direction. The struggle is real before the discipline even begins.
translationLiteral: चञ्चलम् (restless) / हि (indeed) / मनः (mind) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / प्रमाथि (turbulent) / बलवत् (strong) / दृढम् (stubborn, firm) । / तस्य (of that) / अहम् (I) / निग्रहम् (control, restraint) / मन्ये (consider) / वायोः (of wind) / इव (like) / सुदुष्करम् (very difficult)
